prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a wren has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the wren-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a wren, a bep, and a ikk. The wren is underneath the bep. The bep is to-the-left-of the ikk. The wren has a purple button on top, a green button on side, and a red button on bottom. The bep has a green button on top and a purple button on bottom. The ikk has a red button on top and a purple button on side. Pressing the purple button on the wren activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a lurn, a jorp, and a skiv. The jorp is underneath the lurn. The lurn is to-the-left-of the skiv. The lurn has a green button on top and a purple button on side. The jorp has a purple button on top, a green button on side, and a red button on bottom. The skiv has a red button on top and a green button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the wren-analog, and which button activates it?
